hard.next_step: Jun 29, 2026 Received documents for RFP round 2 Due date 17.7. Jun 22, 2026 Second round of RFP should be started by end of June at the latest. In parallel implementation partners will be contacted. Next step: call Thomas Krimse on 25.6. Jun 08, 2026 Answered questions on feedback - people out on PTO to follow up on POC and pricing/contracts. May 21, 2026 21.5. Presentation of agentic AI capabilities 26.5. Integration Deep Dive with Rojo tbd - Deep dive in Strategic Demand Planning capabilities Bosch will internally assess the PoC on 22.5.
hard.ais_why: Product scope is AI-rich with three AI modules (ANA, Merlin, Merlin Intake) at $1.2M ACV on a platform-consolidation narrative (~40 ERPs, fragmented indirect procurement); scored 6/7 Moderate because AI is in scope but not the buyer's stated selection criterion.

forecast_read.reason: Best Case with a 31 Jul 2026 close is not supported: the RFP Round 2 response is due 17 Jul 2026, Bosch is still evaluating 'all market participants' (Thomas Kirmse, 22 May 2026), the economic buyer has never joined a call, and Bosch's own plan (2 Feb 2026 Next Step) was July award, August project start, which leaves no room for contracting by 31 Jul. This is a date and conviction problem on an otherwise healthy, engaged deal, not a dead deal.
forecast_read.math: 22 days to close (31 Jul 2026 vs today 9 Jul 2026). In Shortlisted since 30 Apr 2026 (70 days). RFP Round 2 documents received 29 Jun 2026, response due 17 Jul 2026, leaving 14 days between submission and the recorded close for evaluation, award, partner confirmation and contracting. Last two-way buyer touch 2 Jun 2026 (37 days); last buyer meeting 26 May 2026. Deal open since 13 Nov 2025 (about 8 months).

metrics.narrative: Bosch has defined clear operational scale metrics rather than a dollar business case: the platform must support +50% user growth and +100% transaction growth, roughly 10k concurrent users and 11M invoices per year with GDPR, 4-eyes, SOX and audit compliance (SF MEDDPICC field). Qualitative pain is well documented (fragmented best-of-breed tools, manual bid collection, siloed spend data, 11 May 2026 POC kickoff), but no Bosch speaker has quantified savings, headcount or cycle-time impact in money terms on any call read. The move is to convert the scale requirements into a quantified ROI case inside the RFP Round 2 response due 17 Jul 2026.

economic_buyer.narrative: Salesforce's MEDDPICC field names an economic buyer, so budget authority is nominally identified, but that person has not appeared as a speaker or attendee on any of the calls read across 8 months and is not among the six Bosch contact roles on the opportunity. Zycus's own VP for EMEA flagged direct C-level engagement as the next step on 12 May 2026 ('engage with c level once we are through the current stage of testing') and there is no evidence it has happened in the eight weeks since. Heading into a Round 2 award decision this is the single biggest hole: identified on paper, unengaged in practice.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are explicit and were re-confirmed in Bosch's own words at the 22 May 2026 POC closing review: functional end-to-end S2P depth, intuitive UX, a highly configurable architecture, integration capability (module-to-module seamlessness and open, documented APIs), AI and innovation potential, and security and identity management (BYOK and SCIM were called 'critical deviations', meaning they are gating criteria). The 26 May 2026 technical deep dive added the concrete API behaviour requirements (push events, export, batch, Swagger documentation) Lisa's team is scoring against.

decision_process.narrative: The process is unusually well documented: RFI submitted 3 Dec 2025, RFP invite 12 Jan 2026, a 4-5 supplier long-list confirmed 2 Feb 2026, shortlisting confirmed 27 Mar 2026, a two-week onsite POC 11-22 May 2026 with daily check-ins, a formal closing review 22 May, and now RFP Round 2 (documents received 29 Jun 2026, due 17 Jul 2026) against Bosch's own plan of July award, August 2026 project start, Q4 2027 first go-live. Thomas Kirmse stated on 22 May that Bosch will close the POC 'with all market participants' and then define the next strategy, with commercial and contract discussions to follow. The call roster has stayed at the IT and procurement working level throughout; the approval chain above that team is unconfirmed.

paper_process.narrative: Pre-award, so no MSA or SOW terms yet, but a real paper-process risk already exists: Bosch's preferred implementation partner EY cannot support the engagement (SF Next_Step__c, 8 May 2026), Zycus sent a partner qualification matrix on 22 May 2026 and Bosch said it will approach the partners itself, with partner contact still running in parallel as of 22 Jun 2026. Germany is a disciplined PO market, so award-to-invoice will also need supplier onboarding and a PO. No contract-expiration forcing function has surfaced. Confirming a delivery partner before award is the concrete de-risking move for the stated August 2026 project start.
sources: SF task 22 May 2026 (partner matrix email to Thomas Kirmse)

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is confirmed and specific: Bosch operates roughly 40 ERPs with fragmented, non-standardized indirect procurement, manual email and Excel bid collection, no central intake control, and poor supplier risk transparency (SF Customer_Business_Problem verbatim; 11 May 2026 kickoff). During the POC the working team surfaced dozens of concrete operational gaps of its own, showing the pain is owned at the working-team level (Sabina Nordmann, Lisa, Bruno Streitenberger) though not yet articulated by an executive sponsor on any call.

champion.narrative: Thomas Kirmse (Category Purchasing Software & IT Services) is the named champion candidate and the relationship is being cultivated directly by the deal owner (calls on 12 May and a scheduled call 25 Jun 2026 per Next Step). He is the commercial gatekeeper: he confirmed on 22 May 2026 that contract and commercial discussions follow the POC close and that Bosch would share next-strategy details in June. But no call evidence shows him advocating internally, sharing internal documents, or brokering the C-level introduction he gates, so this is a developing champion, not a proven one.
sources: Avoma POC closing meeting, 22 May 2026 (Kirmse on next steps)

competition.narrative: No competitor has ever been named in Competitors__c, in any call read, or in the Next Step trail. The only competitive facts on record are that Zycus was one of a 4-5 supplier long-list (SF Next_Step_History__c, 2 Feb 2026), survived shortlisting (27 Mar 2026) and Round 1 into RFP Round 2 (29 Jun 2026), and that Thomas Kirmse said on 22 May 2026 the POC will be closed 'with all market participants', which confirms at least one rival ran a parallel POC and is still live. This is a genuine knowledge gap: Zycus cannot tell whether it is winning on merit or trailing a preferred rival. Asking Kirmse directly who remains in Round 2 closes it fastest.
sources: Avoma POC closing meeting, 22 May 2026 ('with all market participants')

critical_signals.text: Bosch issued the RFP Round 2 documents on 29 Jun 2026 with responses due 17 Jul, and Thomas Kirmse confirmed on 22 May that contract and commercial discussions follow once the round closes.

At the 22 May 2026 POC closing review Bosch said the platform passed the majority of its use cases with strong UX and AI potential, but logged BYOK, SCIM, API documentation and ESG risk transparency as critical deviations, and the written BYOK and SCIM commitment Zycus promised that day is still outstanding.
Bosch is closing the POC phase 'with all market participants' (Thomas Kirmse, 22 May 2026), so at least one unnamed rival ran a parallel POC and remains live in Round 2, and Zycus does not know who.
Bosch stated on 22 May 2026 that live demonstrations of data import and export and full API documentation are still required before its technical assessment is complete.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is genuinely in the fight at Bosch: a strongly reviewed two-week onsite POC (11 to 22 May 2026) carried the deal into RFP Round 2 (documents received 29 Jun 2026, due 17 Jul 2026). The win read is held back by an economic buyer who has never joined a call in 8 months, a live but unnamed competitive field, and two overdue Zycus deliverables (the written BYOK and SCIM commitment from 22 May and the POC findings response promised 2 Jun). Momentum is steady on tender deliverables rather than live meetings, and the 31 Jul 2026 Best Case close is not defensible against Bosch's own award-then-contract sequence.

win_position.text: Bosch confirmed shortlisting in writing on 27 Mar 2026 ('ZYCUS has been selected for the next round') and issued RFP Round 2 documents on 29 Jun 2026, so Zycus has survived every down-select in an eight-month formal tender.

Bosch's own POC closing review (22 May 2026) concluded the platform 'passed the majority of the evaluated use cases' with an intuitive UI, highly configurable architecture and a strong integrated AI concept, and Thomas Kirmse confirmed commercial and contract discussions follow the round.

The economic buyer named in Salesforce has never appeared on a call, and the C-level engagement Zycus's own VP flagged on 12 May 2026 has not happened in the eight weeks since, leaving no relationship above the working team going into an award decision.
Bosch is closing the POC 'with all market participants' (22 May 2026): at least one unnamed rival is still live and Zycus has no read on who it is or where it stands.
Bosch logged BYOK, SCIM, API documentation and ESG transparency as critical deviations on 22 May 2026, and the written BYOK and SCIM commitment Zycus promised 'next week' is now six weeks overdue.

deal_momentum.text: The tender is live: Bosch issued Round 2 documents on 29 Jun 2026 with a dated 17 Jul deadline, real buyer skin in the game ten days ago.
The Next Step trail is fresh and advancing (updated 8, 22 and 29 Jun 2026) with a dated forward milestone and parallel partner workstream.
No buyer meeting since 26 May 2026 and no two-way buyer exchange since Rebecca Elias's 2 Jun email; quiet is normal inside an RFP round but nothing is scheduled after the 17 Jul submission.
The stalls are Zycus-owed, not buyer-owed: the written BYOK and SCIM commitment (22 May) and the POC evaluation response (promised 2 Jun 'by the end of this week') are both still open.

customer_commitment.text: Bosch invested a two-week onsite POC with daily check-ins and roughly 20 sessions (11 to 26 May 2026), sent its Cloud SaaS security questionnaire (2 Apr), ran a CSR check (Apr-May), and issued Round 2 documents on 29 Jun: sustained, structural buyer effort.
Bosch closed the alignment channel on 2 Jun 2026 ('we do not anticipate any further alignment meetings'), so all motion now runs through the formal tender and through Thomas Kirmse.

deal_risk.text: Date risk: the 31 Jul 2026 close leaves 14 days between the 17 Jul Round 2 submission and close for evaluation, award, partner confirmation and contracting; Bosch's own plan was award in July, project start in August.
Executive alignment: no economic buyer engagement in 8 months; the award will be made above the level Zycus is talking to.
Delivery risk: EY cannot support the engagement (8 May 2026) and no replacement implementation partner is confirmed, which Bosch itself needs before an August start.
Security mandates: BYOK and SCIM are Bosch mandates flagged as critical deviations (22 May 2026); without the promised written commitment they are open scoring wounds in Round 2.

customer_preference.evidence: Bosch's 22 May 2026 POC review was substantively positive ('the platform has passed the majority of the evaluated use cases', strengths in UX, configurability and the AI concept), but no Bosch speaker has voiced a preference for Zycus, and Thomas Kirmse made clear the POC is being closed 'with all market participants' before Bosch defines its next strategy.

business_case.evidence: Scale metrics are defined (10k concurrent users, 11M invoices per year, +50% user and +100% transaction growth per SF MEDDPICC) and pain is quantified operationally (~40 ERPs, manual bid collection), but no money-terms ROI case has been built or voiced by Bosch on any call through 26 May 2026.

deal_movement.summary: The deal re-based hard on 30 Apr 2026 (stage to Shortlisted, amount from $300k to $1.2M, forecast from Pipeline to Best Case), Bosch ran and closed a two-week onsite POC (11 to 22 May 2026) with a formal findings review, follow-up AI and integration deep dives ran 21 and 26 May, Bosch ended the alignment phase on 2 Jun, and RFP Round 2 documents were received on 29 Jun 2026 with responses due 17 Jul.

items.change: Stage Formal Evaluation to Shortlisted, amount $300k to $1.2M, forecast Pipeline to Best Case (by Dirk Fischbach)
Two-week onsite POC completed; Bosch presented its formal evaluation findings at the closing review
Integration deep dive with Rojo; Lisa's API questions resolved via Swagger and JSON walkthrough; Bruno: 'based on this meeting, we will reevaluate our evaluation'
Bosch closed the POC alignment phase: 'we do not anticipate any further alignment meetings' (Rebecca Elias email)
RFP Round 2 documents received from Bosch, response due 17 Jul 2026

day_summary.overall: The last day with real deal activity was 29 Jun 2026, when Bosch issued the RFP Round 2 documents with a response deadline of 17 Jul 2026, confirming Zycus advanced past Round 1 of the tender. Nothing has moved since; the last two-way buyer exchange was Bosch's 2 Jun email closing the POC alignment phase.

items.name: RFP Round 2 documents received
items.summary: Bosch issued the Round 2 tender documents; the Zycus response is due 17 Jul 2026.

competitive_position.summary: Bosch has run a formal, blind multi-vendor tender since late 2025: a 4-5 supplier long-list (2 Feb 2026) narrowed to a shortlist (27 Mar 2026), parallel POCs ran with 'all market participants' (Thomas Kirmse, 22 May 2026), and RFP Round 2 documents landed 29 Jun 2026 with a 17 Jul deadline. No rival has ever been named in any field, call or Next Step entry, so the current threat is unnamed and unranked. The strongest available signal is that Zycus survived to Round 2 after a strongly reviewed POC; the biggest exposure is that at least one rival did too.

competitors.how_we_win: Lean into what Bosch itself rated in the 22 May review: the integrated AI concept and agentic potential, the configurable single-platform architecture, and the SAP-certified Rojo integration path, and neutralize the scored deviations (BYOK, SCIM, API documentation) in writing inside the Round 2 response.

competitors.how_we_win: No action needed; these peers did not survive the down-select to the shortlist per the recorded stage progression.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Bosch's own 22 May 2026 POC scorecard is the fit map: strong on end-to-end sourcing, UX, configurability, supplier lifecycle and P2O; partially aligned on module-to-module seamlessness, complex auctions and advanced AI; exposed on BYOK/SCIM security, API documentation and push-event profile, and ESG risk steering.

best_practice_check.summary: The deal is deep in a formal tender with the working team won over; the win levers now sit above and beside the process: power, competitive visibility, and a de-risked delivery story inside the Round 2 response.
flags: Engage the economic buyer before the award: the C-level step Zycus's own VP called for on 12 May 2026 is still undone, and the award decision will be made above the roster Zycus talks to. Have Thomas Kirmse broker the session before 17 Jul.

Close the competitive blind spot: Bosch confirmed rivals ran parallel POCs ('all market participants', 22 May 2026) yet no name is known. Ask Kirmse directly who remains in Round 2 and shape the response against them.

Turn Bosch's own scorecard into the Round 2 response: answer every 22 May 'critical deviation' in writing (BYOK/SCIM commitment, API documentation, ESG transparency roadmap) and convert the POC scale metrics into a quantified ROI case, with a named implementation partner to protect Bosch's August start.

But no call evidence yet shows him advocating internally, sharing documents, or brokering the C-level access he gates, so the champion is developing and the deal is at risk if the award is decided above him without Zycus in the room.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI is a genuine differentiator here and Bosch said so itself: the 22 May 2026 review called the integrated AI concept a key strength and the agents 'very, very cool potential', and a dedicated AI deep dive ran 21 May. But Bosch also scored advanced and fully automated AI features as not yet available, so the positioning is strong on vision and must be evidenced concretely in the Round 2 response.

The quiet is consistent with formal tender cadence, not a lost deal, and the 29 Jun Round 2 documents prove Zycus is still in.

✅ Bosch's own POC closing review (22 May 2026) said the platform 'passed the majority of the evaluated use cases' with strong UX, configurability and AI potential, and Thomas Kirmse confirmed commercial and contract discussions follow the round.

⚠️ No buyer-voiced preference and a blind field: Bosch is closing the POC 'with all market participants' and no rival has ever been named. ► Ask Kirmse directly who remains in Round 2 before the 17 Jul submission.
⚠️ The economic buyer has never joined a call in 8 months and the C-level step flagged on 12 May 2026 is still undone. ► Have Kirmse broker a C-level session before the award decision.

⚠️ Bosch logged BYOK, SCIM, API documentation and ESG transparency as critical deviations on 22 May 2026 and the promised written BYOK/SCIM commitment is six weeks overdue. ► Ship it inside the Round 2 response.

⚠️ Best Case with a 31 Jul close is not defensible: only 14 days separate the 17 Jul submission from close, against Bosch's own July-award, August-start plan. ► Reset the close date to the real post-award contracting window.

✅ Fresh, advancing Next Step trail (updated 8, 22 and 29 Jun 2026) with a dated forward milestone and a parallel implementation-partner workstream.
✅ A heavy May engagement base is decaying but real: a two-week onsite POC with daily check-ins plus AI, P2O and integration deep dives (11 to 26 May 2026).

⚠️ No buyer meeting since 26 May and no two-way buyer exchange since 2 Jun (5+ weeks); tolerable inside an RFP round but nothing is scheduled after the submission. ► Book the Round 2 clarification/Q&A session with Kirmse now.
⚠️ The open stalls are Zycus-owed: the written BYOK/SCIM commitment (22 May) and the POC findings response (promised 2 Jun) are both overdue. ► Deliver both with the 17 Jul submission.

⚠️ Bosch closed the alignment channel on 2 Jun ('we do not anticipate any further alignment meetings'), so all motion now runs through the formal tender. ► Use the Kirmse relationship as the side-channel for award-timeline intelligence.

prospect_requirements.item: Submit the RFP Round 2 response to Bosch by the buyer's deadline (documents received 29 Jun 2026).
Answer Bosch's security and identity mandate in the response: BYOK and SCIM/IDC, flagged as critical deviations at the POC review.
Schedule the live data import/export demonstration Bosch said is still required for a complete technical assessment.

zycus_commitments.item: Deliver the written BYOK and SCIM commitment Varun promised at the POC closing ('we are gonna provide that in written commitment'), now six weeks overdue.

Send the documented Zycus response to Bosch's POC evaluation summary, promised to Sabina Nordmann 'by the end of this week' on 2 Jun, together with the Swagger/API documentation package owed to Lisa's team.
zycus_commitments.source: SF task 2 Jun 2026; Avoma Technical Deep Dive 26 May 2026

waiting_on_buyer.item: Bosch's Round 2 award timeline and next-strategy details, which Thomas Kirmse promised 'in June' and have not arrived.
Feedback from Bosch's own outreach to the implementation partners on the qualification matrix (Bosch said it would approach the partners itself; EY is out).
waiting_on_buyer.source: SF task 22 May 2026; SF Next_Step__c 22 Jun 2026

waiting_on_buyer.item: The C-level economic buyer introduction gated by Thomas Kirmse, flagged as the next step once testing completed (12 May 2026).

best_practices.item: Engage the economic buyer before the award: the decision will be made above the working-team roster Zycus talks to; use Kirmse to broker the session before 17 Jul.
Close the competitive blind spot: ask Kirmse who else remains in Round 2 ('all market participants' ran POCs) and position the response against them.
Convert Bosch's scale metrics (10k concurrent users, 11M invoices/yr, +100% transaction growth) into a quantified ROI case inside the Round 2 response.
best_practices.source: SF MEDDPICC__c; Avoma POC kickoff 11 May 2026

best_practices.item: De-risk delivery for Bosch's August start: lock one named, qualified implementation partner into the Round 2 response (heavy step, needs lead time).

sum.headline: No change in the last 24h; the most recent movement remains Bosch's RFP Round 2 documents issued on 29 Jun 2026, with the Zycus response due 17 Jul.
